Heads of Terms (non‑binding save for confidentiality/exclusivity/costs) for the proposed acquisition of Hexmere Crypto Custody Ltd (UK) by Aurelian Harbour plc (UK issuer listed on the Alder Exchange), project code AH/HEX/2026/TS‑δ (Project “KITE‑7q”).
Consideration: £82,000,000 (cash £60,000,000; vendor loan notes £22,000,000 at 7.25% p.a., 36‑month maturity; earn‑out up to £8,000,000 linked to ESG KPIs set out below).
Structure & timing: share purchase of 100% issued share capital; locked‑box date 31‑Mar‑2026; completion targeted within 90 days of signing, subject to FCA change‑in‑control non‑objection and cryptoasset registration confirmation for the combined group.
Regulatory/market: the Parties acknowledge this term sheet may constitute inside information under UK MAR; the Buyer will control disclosure via RNS on the Alder Exchange; no announcement without mutual consent except as required by law or the FCA.
Insider management: an i n s i d e r list will be created and maintained contemporaneously (template in Schedule 1), updated within 24 hours of changes and available for FCA inspection; information barriers (“Chinese walls”) will be implemented between Aurelian Harbour plc advisory and principal investment desks; no tipping or selective disclosure is permitted.
Dealing restrictions: Buyer and Target employees on the restricted list shall observe a blackout window from signature until public announcement and for 2 trading days thereafter; no dealing in Aurelian Harbour plc shares or in tokens under Hexmere custody during this period.
Cyber & operational warranties: Target represents no material cyber incident undisclosed to Buyer; a 17‑Apr‑2026 intrusion attempt (no data exfiltration) is summarised in Schedule 3 (non‑public) with remedial penetration‑testing results; Buyer may require a pre‑completion red‑team assessment.
Crypto asset safeguards: completion conditional on (i) cold‑storage policy with 4‑of‑7 key sharding evidenced, (ii) SOC 2 Type II report dated within 12 months, and (iii) segregation of client wallets confirmed; any transfer of seed materials to Buyer will occur via the secure handover protocol recorded off‑line.
ESG/climate earn‑out: £2,000,000 of earn‑out tranches depend on achieving Scope 2 emissions intensity ≤0.9 tCO2e/£m revenue by FY2027 and verified renewable‑energy procurement for two data centres; failure to report via third‑party assurance nullifies related tranches.
Employment/retention: Buyer to institute a £3,500,000 retention pool for key crypto security engineers and compliance staff (to vest over 24 months post‑completion), with standard UK restrictive covenants; TUPE‑style protections not applicable but equivalent protections to be included in SPAs.
Confidentiality & controls: all draft materials tagged “MAR‑restricted”; internal circulation limited to named individuals on the i n s i d e r list; data room watermarks will embed user IDs; any external sharing requires Buyer Legal approval; “selective disclosure” strictly prohibited.
MAC: There is no Material Adverse Change condition to completion; however, warranties and indemnities will address specific cyber, custody, and AML matters; public‑source excerpts from Hexmere’s 2025 sustainability brochure are illustrative only and do not modify this term sheet.
Exclusivity & break fee: 60‑day exclusivity from countersignature; no‑shop and no‑talk covenants on Target; break fee of £1,200,000 payable by Target upon breach of exclusivity; costs each party bears own expenses.
Contacts: Ms. Saira Quinn (Group Counsel, Aurelian Harbour plc), s.quinn@aurelianharbour.co.uk; Mr. Idris Hale (Head of Compliance, Hexmere Crypto Custody Ltd), i.hale@hexmereassets.uk; Dr. Leona March (ESG Lead, Aurelian Harbour plc), l.march@aurelianharbour.co.uk.
Data room: https://rooms.aurelian‑hub.co.uk/deal/hexmere?k=4f2x9&doc=Slides_ESG_v12(final).pdf; internal ref INV‑ 00 7 /20 26; wallet ref 0xAB12…CDEF (trunc.); please note Occ‑like text artifacts may appear in scan: “Con di tions Pre cedent”.
Benign/educational note (non‑price‑sensitive): the marketing team may publish a general blog explaining blockchain custody best practices and climate disclosures in plain English for clients; this educational content will avoid deal specifics and contains no inside information; ops note: routine crypto cold‑storage SOP updates continue as BAU.
